what_it_does: Records an attachment entry that links the design-assets channel to an image asset at image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Upload, typed as images, with an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Target is an image:// URI pointing to icons/Icon_Upload
added timestamp is an ISO 8601 UTC value
standouts: Target URI uses the image:// scheme for an icon asset
It is a minimal, typed attachment record for the design-assets channel
conventions: Follows a ledger-style attachment schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
References assets by a URI rather than embedding them
concerns: No human-readable name or description for the attachment
The image:// URI cannot be resolved or verified from this file alone
No information about who added it or why
notes: This is a sparse attachment ledger entry that points the design-assets channel at an upload icon image asset.
questions: What does Icon_Upload depict or identify?
Where is the actual image file located and how is the image:// URI resolved?
Who added this attachment and for what specific use case?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it records an image attachment reference for a channel.
intention_signals: It appears to be registering an upload icon image under the design-assets channel so the asset can be discovered and referenced by that channel.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and a specific icon image asset, supporting asset lookup and provenance tracking.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment ledger entry att-d4d31d05 in the design-assets channel records an images attachment targeting image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Upload with an added ISO timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to catalog an upload icon image asset available to the design-assets channel.
